content: Reflexion’s persistent dynamic self-reflection memory can fill a key blind spot in the vanilla Self-Refine framework: by storing historical self-feedback traces from past refinement iterations, it can help Self-Refine avoid repeating the same ineffective self-correction cycles when refining LLM outputs. This creates a hybrid framework that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision, while gaining the long-term reflective context that makes refinement more efficient.
